No outward appearance or change can be acceptable to God as long as any idol holds a place in the heart; yet how many choose their own plans and their own righteousness instead of the way of salvation! People's corruptions are idols in their hearts, and they create them themselves; God will allow them to run their course. Sin makes the sinner offensive in the eyes of the pure and holy God; and also in his own eyes, whenever conscience is awakened. Let us seek to be cleansed from the guilt and pollution of sins, in that fountain which the Lord has opened.

National sins bring national judgments. Though sinners escape one judgment, another awaits them. When God’s professing people rebel against him, they may expect all his judgments. Noah's faith, obedience, and prayers saved his family, but not the old world. Job’s sacrifice and prayer on behalf of his friends were accepted, and Daniel prevailed to save his companions and the wise men of Babylon. But a people that has filled the measure of their sins should not expect to escape for the sake of any righteous people living among them; not even of the most distinguished saints, who can be accepted in their own case only through the sufferings and righteousness of Christ. Yet even when God makes the greatest devastation by his judgments, he saves some to be reminders of his mercy. With a firm belief that we will approve of all of God’s dealings with ourselves and with all mankind, let us silence all rebellious murmurs and objections.
